✦ Recovery Ministry Partner
Transformation Life Center
"Transform Men to Transform the World"

Calvary Baptist Church is deeply connected to TLC — a Christian discipleship and recovery ministry dedicated to restoring the lives of men struggling with addiction and life-controlling issues through the transforming power of Jesus Christ.

Learn More About TLC → Visit TLC Website →
📖
Bible-Based Discipleship
Daily immersion in Scripture, prayer, and worship as the foundation of transformation
🤝
Accountability & Community
Brotherhood, mentorship, and structured support for lasting recovery
✝
Gospel-Centered Hope
The power of Jesus Christ to set people free and build a new life
Are you or someone you love struggling with addiction?
You do not have to face it alone. Help is available. Transformation is possible.
Find Help Through TLC → Talk to Pastor Chris →

Who We Are

About Calvary Baptist

A community of faith rooted in Scripture, committed to one another, and sent to serve Medford and beyond.

Our Pastor
Pastor Chris Pandolfi
Senior Pastor · Calvary Baptist Church

Pastor Chris Pandolfi and his wife Sheila lead Calvary Baptist Church with a deep commitment to expository preaching, discipleship, and the Medford community.

Under his leadership, Calvary Baptist has remained steadfast in its mission — to know Christ, be conformed into His image, and to make Him known throughout Greater Medford and beyond.

Pastor Chris can be reached at PastorChris@calvaryli.org

"All Scripture is breathed out by God and profitable for teaching, for reproof, for correction, and for training in righteousness, that the man of God may be complete, equipped for every good work."

2 Timothy 3:16-17, ESV
Our Story
History of Calvary Baptist
THE BEGINNING
Calvary Baptist Church Founded
Calvary Baptist Church was established in Medford, Long Island with a vision to reach the greater community with the gospel of Jesus Christ.
GROWTH
Expanding Our Reach
The congregation grows and expands its ministries, adding Sunday School programs, children's ministry, and community outreach efforts throughout Medford and Long Island.
TODAY
Pastor Chris Pandolfi Leads Calvary
Under Pastor Chris's leadership, Calvary Baptist continues to grow in faith, community, and outreach — committed to knowing Christ and making Him known throughout Greater Medford.
Our Mission
Why We Exist

Calvary Baptist Church exists to glorify God by knowing Christ, being conformed into His image, and making Him known — in Medford, on Long Island, and to the ends of the earth.

We are a Bible-believing, gospel-preaching congregation committed to expository preaching, biblical discipleship, and loving our community in the name of Jesus Christ.

"Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you."

Matthew 28:19-20, ESV

What We Believe
Our Statement of Faith
The Holy Scriptures
We teach that the Bible is God's written revelation to man — the sixty-six books given to us by the Holy Spirit, verbally inspired in every word, absolutely inerrant, infallible, and God-breathed. It is the only infallible rule of faith and practice.
God
We teach that there is but one living and true God — an infinite, all-knowing Spirit, perfect in all His attributes, one in essence, eternally existing in three Persons: Father, Son, and Holy Spirit — each equally deserving worship and obedience.
Jesus Christ
We teach that Jesus Christ is the second Person of the Trinity, fully God and fully man. He was born of a virgin, lived a sinless life, died on the cross for our sins, and rose bodily from the dead. He is the only mediator between God and man.
The Holy Spirit
We teach that the Holy Spirit is a divine Person, coequal with the Father and Son. He convicts, regenerates, indwells, sanctifies, and empowers believers for service, and seals them unto the day of redemption.
Salvation
We teach that salvation is wholly of God by grace, on the basis of the redemption of Jesus Christ and the merit of His shed blood — not on the basis of human merit or works. It is received through repentance and faith in Christ alone.
The Church
We teach that all who place their faith in Jesus Christ are immediately placed by the Holy Spirit into the Body of Christ. The local church is a body of baptized believers gathered for worship, discipleship, and mission.
Calvary Baptist Church
"To Know Christ, To Be Conformed into His Image and to Make Him Known."
2981 Horseblock Road
Medford, NY 11763
PastorChris@calvaryli.org

Give to Calvary Baptist

Your generosity fuels Bible teaching, community outreach, missions, and every ministry at Calvary. Every gift makes a difference.

Give Online via PushPay Questions? Contact Us

Ways to Give
How to Support Calvary
📱
Give Online
Secure, instant giving through PushPay. One-time or recurring gifts. All major cards and bank transfers accepted.
💬
Give by Text
Text the word GIVE to 833-921-6783 and follow the simple prompts to complete your gift in seconds.
✉️
Give by Mail
Send a check payable to Calvary Baptist Church · 2981 Horseblock Road, Medford NY 11763
🏛️
Give in Person
Offering is received during Sunday morning and evening services each week at 2981 Horseblock Road.
Your Impact
Where Your Gift Goes

Every dollar given to Calvary Baptist supports expository preaching, children's and youth ministry, local community outreach, international missions, and the daily operations of our church family in Medford.

Adult & Children Education

✝ Sunday School

Sunday School at Calvary Baptist is offered for all ages every Sunday at 10:00 AM. Our classes provide Bible teaching in an engaging, age-appropriate setting for children, youth, and adults alike.

← Back to All Ministries
About This Ministry
Sunday School

Sunday School at Calvary Baptist is offered for all ages every Sunday at 10:00 AM. Our classes provide Bible teaching in an engaging, age-appropriate setting for children, youth, and adults alike.

We believe that learning God's Word together is foundational to the Christian life. Our Sunday School program equips believers of all ages to grow in their knowledge of Scripture and their walk with Christ.

What to expect:
Engaging Bible-centered teaching, small group discussion, and fellowship with other believers in a warm and welcoming environment.
Schedule
Every Sunday · 10:00 AM · Main Building

Pastor Chris Pandolfi

Kids Church

Children's Ministry

Bringing children to a clear and accurate saving knowledge of Christ.

← Back to All Ministries
About This Ministry
Children's Ministry

The Children's Ministry at Calvary Baptist Church exists to glorify God by providing children with a Sunday School that provides both Bible teaching and engaging activities. The intent being to bring children to a clear and accurate saving knowledge of Christ.

Sunday School meets during the regular Sunday service. We use the Word of Life curriculum — a proven, Gospel-centered approach to teaching children the truths of Scripture.

Schedule
Every Sunday during the 11:00 AM Morning Service · Children's Ministry Area

© 2026 Calvary Baptist Church of Medford, NY
Worship & Choir

Music Ministry

Led by Mike Gryzlo, our choir and worship team are committed to leading the congregation in meaningful, Christ-honoring worship.

← Back to All Ministries
About This Ministry
Music Ministry

Our Music Ministry is led by Mike Gryzlo, our Music Director. The choir and worship team are committed to leading the congregation in meaningful, Christ-honoring worship each Sunday through Bible-based music and song.

Experience the joy of worship through music and song. Our services are filled with Bible-based messages and Christ-honoring music. We are a very warm and loving local body of believers.

Interested in Joining?

We welcome singers and musicians who have a heart for worship and a desire to serve the congregation.

Contact Us →
View All Ministries →
© 2026 Calvary Baptist Church of Medford, NY
Local & Global Outreach

Missions

Calvary Baptist Church is committed to reaching the Greater Medford Community and the world with the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

← Back to All Ministries
Our Supported Missionaries
Those We Send & Support

We accomplish the Great Commission through prayer, financial support, and partnership with missionaries serving locally and around the world.

Carlos & Evelyn Ruiz
Loja, Ecuador

Carlos and Evelyn Ruiz serve the Lord in Loja, Ecuador through SIMUSA. Calvary Baptist is proud to partner with and support their ministry in South America.

Support Their Ministry →
Raffaele Spitale
Torino, Italy

Raffaele Spitale serves in Torino, Italy through Chiesa Solo Cristo. Calvary Baptist supports his gospel work in Europe.

Visit Church Website → Support His Ministry →
Chosen Peoples Ministries
Jewish Outreach

Chosen People Ministries exists to pray for, evangelize, disciple, and serve Jewish people everywhere and to help fellow believers do the same. Calvary Baptist is honored to support their vital work.

Support This Ministry →
David Huizing
Word of Life

David Huizing serves with Word of Life, reaching young people with the Gospel through camps, clubs, and discipleship programs. Calvary Baptist is proud to partner with and support his ministry.

Support His Ministry →
🌍
Transformation Life Center
Local Outreach · Drug Recovery Network

Transformation Life Center (TLC) is a faith-based drug and alcohol recovery program that Calvary Baptist is connected to and supports. TLC provides life-changing residential and outpatient recovery services rooted in the Gospel of Jesus Christ.

Visit TLC Website →

Midweek Fellowship

📖 Wednesday Bible Study

Every Wednesday evening at 7 PM, Calvary Baptist gathers in the Fellowship Hall for an in-depth study of God's Word. All are welcome — no prior Bible knowledge is required.

← Back to All Ministries
About This Ministry
Wednesday Bible Study

Every Wednesday evening at 7 PM, Calvary Baptist gathers in the Fellowship Hall for an in-depth study of God's Word. All are welcome — no prior Bible knowledge is required.

Our Wednesday Bible Study is a time of teaching, discussion, and fellowship as we dig deeper into Scripture together. Pastor Chris leads the congregation through books of the Bible and topical studies throughout the year.

What to bring:

Just yourself and your Bible. We provide all study materials. Come as you are — this is a casual, welcoming environment for believers at every stage of their faith journey.
Schedule
Every Wednesday · 7:30 PM · Fellowship Hall · 2981 Horseblock Rd

About This Ministry
Family Ministry

The Family Ministry at Calvary Baptist is dedicated to strengthening and encouraging families in their walk with Christ. Through events, programs, and community, we come alongside families at every stage of life.

From our annual Family Fun Day to Vacation Bible School and holiday events, Calvary creates meaningful opportunities for families to grow together in faith and build lasting friendships.

Recommended Reading
Books
📚
The Reason for God
Timothy Keller addresses frequent doubts that skeptics and believers have about religion. Essential reading for every Christian. Find on Amazon →
📚
The Explicit Gospel
Matt Chandler — a clear and compelling presentation of the Gospel in its fullness. Find on Amazon →
📚
Celebration of Discipline
Richard Foster — a classic guide to the spiritual disciplines that shape a Christ-centered life. Find on Amazon →
📚
How to Read the Bible
Gordon Fee & Douglas Stuart — essential guide to understanding and interpreting Scripture accurately. Find on Amazon →
Strengthening Families
Marriage Resources
💑
The Five Love Languages
Dr. Gary Chapman — understanding how you and your spouse give and receive love. Learn More →
💑
The Meaning of Marriage
Timothy Keller — a biblical and practical look at what marriage is and how to build a strong one. Find on Amazon →
💬
Need Counseling?
Calvary can connect you with trusted Christian counselors in the Greater Medford area. Contact Pastor Chris →

Apologetics
🎯
Reasonable Faith
Dr. William Lane Craig — articulate, intelligent defense of the Christian faith. Visit Site →
🎯
The Case for Christ
Lee Strobel — a journalist investigates the evidence for the divinity of Jesus Christ. Find on Amazon →
🎯
Bible Project
Free animated videos explaining every book of the Bible and major biblical themes. Watch Free →

We're Here For You

Prayer Request

Submit your prayer request and our pastoral team will pray with you. You are not alone.

Submit a Request
How Can We Pray For You?

✓ Thank you. Pastor Chris and our team will be praying for you.
God Hears You
You Are Not Alone

At Calvary Baptist, we believe in the power of prayer. Whether you're facing a health crisis, a broken relationship, a financial struggle, or simply need someone to stand with you — we are here.

Your request will be received by Pastor Chris and our pastoral team. We will pray over every request submitted.

"Do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God."

Philippians 4:6, ESV
Need to Talk?

For urgent pastoral care, reach out to Pastor Chris directly.

PastorChris@calvaryli.org →

Pastoral Note · May 2026
Do We Have a Heart for the Lost?

In Matthew 23, Jesus pours out His lament over Jerusalem — a city that rejected its Messiah. As we studied this passage together, one question lingered: Do we carry the same heart for the lost that Jesus demonstrated?

By Pastor Chris Pandolfi

Women of Calvary

Women's Ministry

Encouraging women to worship and obey our Lord Jesus Christ through the study and application of God's Word.

← Back to All Ministries
About This Ministry
Women's Ministry

Women's Ministry exists at Calvary Baptist Church to encourage women to worship and obey our Lord Jesus Christ through the study and application of God's Word and through relationships with other women for fellowship and accountability.

We believe that women play a vital role in the life of the local church and in the home. Our ministry provides a safe, welcoming space for women to grow in their faith, build meaningful friendships, and support one another through every season of life.

"She opens her mouth with wisdom, and the teaching of kindness is on her tongue."

Proverbs 31:26, ESV

Men of Calvary

Men's Ministry

Equipping and encouraging men to fulfill their God-ordained roles as leaders in the home, the church, and the world.

← Back to All Ministries
About This Ministry
Men's Ministry

The Men's Ministry at Calvary Baptist Church exists to glorify God by equipping and encouraging men to fulfill their God-ordained roles as leaders in the home, the church, and the world.

The men of Calvary meet on Tuesday evenings for fellowship, accountability, and the study of God's Word. Men are also strengthened through personal discipleship and counseling.

Schedule
Every Tuesday at 7:30 PM · Fellowship Hall · 2981 Horseblock Rd

"Be watchful, stand firm in the faith, act like men, be strong. Let all that you do be done in love."

1 Corinthians 16:13-14, ESV
View All Ministries →
© 2026 Calvary Baptist Church of Medford, NY
Doctrine

What We Believe

The Statement of Faith of Calvary Baptist Church of Medford, New York.

The following represents the doctrinal commitments of Calvary Baptist Church. We hold these beliefs not as tradition but as the clear teaching of the Word of God, which we affirm to be inerrant, infallible, and the sole rule of faith and practice.

The Holy Scriptures

We teach that the Bible is God's written revelation to man, and thus the sixty-six books of the Bible given to us by the Holy Spirit constitute the plenary (inspired equally in all parts) Word of God (1 Corinthians 2:7-14; 2 Peter 1:20-21).

We teach that the Word of God is an objective, propositional revelation (1 Thessalonians 2:13; 1 Corinthians 2:13), verbally inspired in every word (2 Timothy 3:16), absolutely inerrant in the original documents, infallible, and God-breathed.

We teach the literal, grammatical, historical interpretation of Scripture which affirms the belief that the opening chapters of Genesis present creation in six literal days (Genesis 1:31; Exodus 31:17), describe the special creation of man and woman (Genesis 1:26-28; 2:5-25), and define marriage as between one man and one woman (Genesis 2:24; Matthew 19:5). Scripture elsewhere dictates that any sexual activity outside of marriage is an abomination before the Lord (Exodus 20:14; Leviticus 18:1-30; Matthew 5:27-32; 19:1-9; 1 Corinthians 5:1-5; 6:9-10; 1 Thessalonians 4:1-7).

We teach that the Bible constitutes the only infallible rule of faith and practice (Matthew 5:18; 24:35; John 10:35; 16:12-13; 17:17; 1 Corinthians 2:13; 2 Timothy 3:15-17; Hebrews 4:12; 2 Peter 1:20-21).

We teach that God spoke in His written Word by a process of dual authorship. The Holy Spirit so superintended the human authors that, through their individual personalities and different styles of writing, they composed and recorded God's Word to man (2 Peter 1:20-21) without error in the whole or in the part (Matthew 5:18; 2 Timothy 3:16).

God

We teach that there is but one living and true God (Deuteronomy 6:4; Isaiah 45:5-7; 1 Corinthians 8:4), an infinite, all-knowing Spirit (John 4:24), perfect in all His attributes, one in essence, eternally existing in three Persons — Father, Son, and Holy Spirit (Matthew 28:19; 2 Corinthians 13:14) — each equally deserving worship and obedience.

God the Father

We teach that God the Father, the first Person of the Trinity, orders and disposes all things according to His own purpose and grace (Psalm 145:8-9; 1 Corinthians 8:6). He is the Creator of all things (Genesis 1:1-31; Ephesians 3:9). As the only absolute and omnipotent Ruler in the universe, He is sovereign in creation, providence, and redemption (Psalm 103:19; Romans 11:36). He has graciously chosen from eternity past those whom He would have as His own (Ephesians 1:4-6); He saves from sin all who come to Him through Jesus Christ; He adopts as His own all those who come to Him (John 1:12; Romans 8:15; Galatians 4:5; Hebrews 12:5-9).

God the Son

We teach that Jesus Christ, the second Person of the Trinity, possesses all the divine excellencies, and in these He is coequal, consubstantial, and co-eternal with the Father (John 10:30; 14:9).

We teach that in the incarnation the eternal Son, without altering His divine nature or surrendering any of the divine attributes, made Himself of no reputation by taking on a full human nature consubstantial with our own, yet without sin (Philippians 2:5-8; Hebrews 4:15; 7:26). He was conceived by the Holy Spirit in the womb of the virgin Mary (Luke 1:35), so that two whole, perfect, and distinct natures, the divine and the human, were joined together in one person. He is therefore very God and very man, yet one Christ, the only mediator between God and man.

We teach that our Lord Jesus Christ accomplished our redemption through the shedding of His blood and sacrificial death on the cross and that His death was voluntary, vicarious, substitutionary, propitiatory, and redemptive (John 10:15; Romans 3:24-25; 5:8; 1 Peter 2:24).

We teach that on the basis of the efficacy of the death of our Lord Jesus Christ, the believing sinner is freed from the punishment, the penalty, the power, and one day the very presence of sin; and that he is declared righteous, given eternal life, and adopted into the family of God (Romans 3:25; 5:8-9; 2 Corinthians 5:14-15; 1 Peter 2:24; 3:18).

We teach that our justification is made sure by His literal, physical resurrection from the dead and that He is now ascended to the right hand of the Father, where He now mediates as our Advocate and High Priest (Matthew 28:6; Luke 24:38-39; Acts 2:30-31; Romans 4:25; 8:34; Hebrews 7:25; 9:24; 1 John 2:1).

We teach that Jesus Christ will return to receive the church, which is His Body, unto Himself at the rapture, and returning with His church in glory, will establish His millennial kingdom on earth (Acts 1:9-11; 1 Thessalonians 4:13-18).

God the Holy Spirit

We teach that the Holy Spirit is a divine Person, eternal, underived, possessing all the attributes of personality and deity including intellect (1 Corinthians 2:10-13), emotions (Ephesians 4:30), will (1 Corinthians 12:11), eternality (Hebrews 9:14), omnipresence (Psalm 139:7-10), omniscience (Isaiah 40:13-14), omnipotence (Romans 15:13), and truthfulness (John 16:13). In all the divine attributes He is coequal and consubstantial with the Father and the Son (Matthew 28:19; Acts 5:3-4; 1 Corinthians 12:4-6; 2 Corinthians 13:14).

We teach that the work of the Holy Spirit in this age began at Pentecost when He came from the Father as promised by Christ (John 14:16-17; 15:26) to initiate and complete the building of the Body of Christ, which is His church (1 Corinthians 12:13). His divine activity includes convicting the world of sin, of righteousness, and of judgment; glorifying the Lord Jesus Christ and transforming believers into the image of Christ (John 16:7-9; Romans 8:29; 2 Corinthians 3:18).

We teach that the Holy Spirit is the supernatural and sovereign Agent in regeneration, baptizing all believers into the Body of Christ (1 Corinthians 12:13). The Holy Spirit also indwells, sanctifies, instructs, empowers them for service, and seals them unto the day of redemption (Romans 8:9; 2 Corinthians 3:6; Ephesians 1:13).

Man

We teach that man was directly and immediately created by God in His image and likeness. Man was created free of sin with a rational nature, intelligence, volition, self-determination, and moral responsibility to God (James 3:9).

Man was also created by God as either male or female, biologically defined and distinct sexes (Genesis 1:27; 2:5-23; 1 Corinthians 11:11-15; Romans 1:26-27) set by God for each individual (Psalm 139:13-14). Confusion between the two is an abomination to Him (Deuteronomy 22:5; Romans 1:26-27; 1 Corinthians 6:9).

We teach that God's intention in the creation of man was that man should glorify God, enjoy God's fellowship, live his life in the will of God, and by this accomplish God's purpose for man in the world (Colossians 1:16; Revelation 4:11).

We teach that in Adam's sin of disobedience to the revealed will and Word of God, man lost his innocence; incurred the penalty of spiritual and physical death; became subject to the wrath of God; and became inherently corrupt and utterly incapable of choosing or doing that which is acceptable to God apart from divine grace (Genesis 2:16-17; 3:1-19; John 3:36; Romans 3:23; 6:23; 1 Corinthians 2:14; Ephesians 2:1-3; 1 Timothy 2:13-14; 1 John 1:8). Man's salvation is thereby wholly of God's grace through the redemptive work of our Lord Jesus Christ.

Salvation

We teach that salvation is wholly of God by grace on the basis of the redemption of Jesus Christ, the merit of His shed blood, and not on the basis of human merit or works (John 1:12; Ephesians 1:7; 2:8-10; 1 Peter 1:18-19).

Regeneration

We teach that regeneration is a supernatural work of the Holy Spirit by which the divine nature and divine life are given (John 3:3-7; Titus 3:5). It is instantaneous and is accomplished solely by the power of the Holy Spirit through the instrumentality of the Word of God (John 5:24), when the repentant sinner, as enabled by the Holy Spirit, responds in faith to the divine provision of salvation. Genuine regeneration is manifested by fruits worthy of repentance as demonstrated in righteous attitudes and conduct (1 Corinthians 6:19-20; Ephesians 2:10).

Election

We teach that election is the act of God by which, before the foundation of the world, He chose in Christ those whom He graciously regenerates, saves, and sanctifies (Romans 8:28-30; Ephesians 1:4-11; 2 Thessalonians 2:13; 2 Timothy 2:10; 1 Peter 1:1-2). We teach that sovereign election does not contradict or negate the responsibility of man to repent and trust Christ as Savior and Lord (Ezekiel 18:23, 32; 33:11; John 3:18-19, 36; 5:40; Romans 9:22-23; Revelation 22:17).

Justification

We teach that justification before God is an act of God (Romans 8:33) by which He declares righteous those who, through faith in Christ, repent of their sins (Luke 13:3; Acts 2:38; 3:19; Romans 2:4; 2 Corinthians 7:10) and confess Him as sovereign Lord (Romans 10:9-10; 1 Corinthians 12:3; Philippians 2:11). This righteousness is apart from any virtue or work of man (Romans 3:20; 4:6) and involves the imputation of our sins to Christ (Colossians 2:14; 1 Peter 2:24) and the imputation of Christ's righteousness to us (1 Corinthians 1:30; 2 Corinthians 5:21).

Sanctification

We teach that every believer is sanctified (set apart) unto God by justification and is therefore declared to be holy (Acts 20:32; 1 Corinthians 1:2, 30; 6:11; Hebrews 2:11; 3:1; 10:10, 14; 13:12; 1 Peter 1:2). We teach that there is also by the work of the Holy Spirit a progressive sanctification by which the state of the believer is brought closer to the standing the believer positionally enjoys through justification. Through obedience to the Word of God and the empowering of the Holy Spirit, the believer is able to live a life of increasing holiness in conformity to the will of God, becoming more and more like our Lord Jesus Christ (John 17:17,19; Romans 6:1-22; 2 Corinthians 3:18; 1 Thessalonians 4:3-4; 5:23).

Security

We teach that all the redeemed once saved are kept by God's power and are thus secure in Christ forever (John 5:24; 6:37-40; 10:27-30; Romans 5:9-10; 8:1, 31-39; 1 Corinthians 1:4-8; Ephesians 4:30; Hebrews 7:25; 13:5; 1 Peter 1:5; Jude 24).

Separation

We teach that separation from sin is clearly called for throughout the Old and New Testaments (2 Corinthians 6:14-7:1; 2 Timothy 3:1-5).

We teach that separation from all religious apostasy and worldly and sinful practices is commanded of us by God (Romans 12:1-2; 1 Corinthians 5:9-13; 2 Corinthians 6:14-7:1; 1 John 2:15-17; 2 John 9-11). We teach that believers should be separated unto our Lord Jesus Christ (2 Thessalonians 1:11-12; Hebrews 12:1-2) and affirm that the Christian life is a life of obedient righteousness and a continual pursuit of holiness (Romans 12:1-2; 2 Corinthians 7:1; Hebrews 12:14; Titus 2:11-14; 1 John 3:1-10).

The Church

We teach that all who place their faith in Jesus Christ are immediately placed by the Holy Spirit into one united spiritual Body, the church (1 Corinthians 12:12-13), the bride of Christ (2 Corinthians 11:2; Ephesians 5:23-32; Revelation 19:7-8), of which Christ is the Head (Ephesians 1:22; 4:15; Colossians 1:18).

We teach that the one supreme authority for the church is Christ (1 Corinthians 11:3; Ephesians 1:22; Colossians 1:18) and that church leadership, gifts, order, discipline, and worship are all appointed through His sovereignty as found in the Scriptures. The biblically designated officers serving under Christ and over the assembly are elders and deacons, both of whom must meet biblical qualifications (1 Timothy 3:1-13; Titus 1:5-9; 1 Peter 5:1-5).

We teach the autonomy of the local church, free from any external authority or control, with the right of self-government (Titus 1:5).

We teach that the purpose of the church is to glorify God (Ephesians 3:21) by building itself up in the faith (Ephesians 4:13-16), by instruction of the Word (2 Timothy 2:2, 15; 3:16-17), by fellowship (Acts 2:47; 1 John 1:3), by keeping the ordinances (Luke 22:19; Acts 2:38-42) and by advancing and communicating the gospel to the entire world (Matthew 28:19; Acts 1:8; 2:42).

We teach that two ordinances have been committed to the local church: baptism and the Lord's Supper (Acts 2:38-42). Christian baptism by immersion (Acts 8:36-39) is the solemn and beautiful testimony of a believer showing forth his faith in the crucified, buried, and risen Savior, and his union with Him in death to sin and resurrection to a new life (Romans 6:1-11). The Lord's Supper is the commemoration and proclamation of His death until He comes, and should be always preceded by solemn self-examination (1 Corinthians 11:28-32).

Last Things

Death

We teach that physical death involves no loss of our immaterial consciousness (Revelation 6:9-11), that the soul of the redeemed passes immediately into the presence of Christ (Luke 23:43; Philippians 1:23; 2 Corinthians 5:8), and that for the redeemed such separation of soul and body will continue until the rapture (1 Thessalonians 4:13-17), when our soul and body will be reunited to be glorified forever with our Lord (Philippians 3:21; 1 Corinthians 15:35-44, 50-54). We teach the bodily resurrection of all men, the saved to eternal life (John 6:39; Romans 8:10-11, 19-23; 2 Corinthians 4:14), and the unsaved to judgment and everlasting punishment (Daniel 12:2; John 5:29; Revelation 20:13-15).

The Rapture of the Church

We teach the personal, bodily return of our Lord Jesus Christ before the seven-year tribulation (1 Thessalonians 4:16; Titus 2:13) to translate His church from this earth (John 14:1-3; 1 Corinthians 15:51-53; 1 Thessalonians 4:15-5:11) and, between this event and His glorious return with His saints, to reward believers according to their works (1 Corinthians 3:11-15; 2 Corinthians 5:10).

The Tribulation Period

We teach that immediately following the removal of the church from the earth the righteous judgments of God will be poured out upon an unbelieving world (Jeremiah 30:7; Daniel 9:27; 12:1; 2 Thessalonians 2:7-12; Revelation 16), and that these judgments will be climaxed by the return of Christ in glory to the earth (Matthew 24:27-31; 25:31-46; 2 Thessalonians 2:7-12).

The Second Coming and the Millennial Reign

We teach that, after the tribulation period, Christ will come to earth to occupy the throne of David (Matthew 25:31; Luke 1:31-33; Acts 1:10-11; 2:29-30) and establish His messianic kingdom for 1,000 years on the earth (Revelation 20:1-7). During this time the resurrected saints will reign with Him over Israel and all the nations of the earth (Ezekiel 37:21-28; Daniel 7:17-22; Revelation 19:11-16).

Eternity

We teach that after the closing of the millennium, the temporary release of Satan, and the judgment of unbelievers (2 Thessalonians 1:9; Revelation 20:7-15), the saved will enter the eternal state of glory with God, after which the elements of this earth are to be dissolved (2 Peter 3:10) and replaced with a new earth, wherein only righteousness dwells (Ephesians 5:5; Revelation 20:15; 21:1-27; 22:1-21). The heavenly city will come down out of heaven and will be the dwelling place of the saints, where they will enjoy forever fellowship with God and one another (John 17:3; Revelation 21-22). Our Lord Jesus Christ, having fulfilled His redemptive mission, will then deliver up the kingdom to God the Father (1 Corinthians 15:24-28), that in all spheres the triune God may reign forever and ever.

Questions About Our Faith?

Pastor Chris is available to discuss any of these doctrinal positions personally.

Contact Pastor Chris →
© 2026 Calvary Baptist Church of Medford, NY
Recovery Ministry

Transformation Life Center

"Transform Men to Transform the World"

Calvary Baptist Church is deeply connected to Transformation Life Center — a Christian discipleship and recovery ministry dedicated to restoring lives of men struggling with addiction and life-controlling issues.

Visit TLC Website → Talk to Pastor Chris →
Location
West Park, New York
Mission
Transform Men to Transform the World
Foundation
Christ-Centered Discipleship
Who They Are
A Ministry of Transformation

Transformation Life Center is a Christian, Biblically-based drug and alcohol recovery ministry that provides the training and the facilities to allow the Holy Spirit to bring His transforming work of moving and walking in newness of life.

TLC is not simply a treatment program. It is a discipleship ministry. Participants are immersed in Bible study, prayer, worship, and mentorship — the goal being not merely sobriety but genuine transformation in Christ.

Calvary Baptist Church is deeply connected to TLC and committed to its mission. We believe that the Gospel of Jesus Christ has the power to set people free from the grip of addiction.

"Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come."

2 Corinthians 5:17, ESV
What TLC Believes
The Bible is the inspired, infallible, authoritative Word of God
There is one God, eternally existent in three persons: Father, Son and Holy Spirit
For salvation, regeneration by the Holy Spirit through faith in Jesus Christ is absolutely essential
The restorative power of Jesus Christ can set people free from the grips of addiction
TLC's Approach
📖
Bible Study & Prayer
Daily immersion in Scripture and prayer as the foundation of recovery
👥
Discipleship & Mentorship
One-on-one mentoring from men who have walked the path to recovery
🏠
Structure & Community
A structured, supportive environment where healing can take place
🛠️
Life Skills & Restoration
Practical skills and tools to build a new life grounded in Christ
Testimonies
Lives Transformed by Christ
"

TLC has provided guidance and structure for my life. One of the best decisions I have made is to follow Jesus and use what TLC has to offer.

— TLC Graduate
"

My time at TLC has given me a better understanding of my Lord and Savior Jesus Christ. And has given me strength to stay sober and one with God.

— TLC Graduate
"

TLC has been instrumental in my walk with the Lord. Recovery is more than overcoming addiction — it is about building a new life filled with purpose and joy.

— TLC Graduate
Are You Struggling?
There Is Hope

If you or someone you love is struggling with addiction or life-controlling issues, you do not have to face it alone. Transformation Life Center exists for this moment.

Calvary Baptist Church is here to walk alongside you. Reach out to Pastor Chris or contact TLC directly. Help is available. Transformation is possible.

Contact TLC Directly → Talk to Pastor Chris →
